description: The Communist Failure in Cuba It is absolutely evident that the socioeconomic transformations imposed by communism in Cuba have caused independently of political slavery an extraor dinary increase of misery and of social injustice And this fact is determined fundamentally by the nature of the system itself and not by the lack of aptitude of those who apply it It Is good to emphasize on this truth because in due time the scientists of Soviet propaganda will try to demonstrate that Cubas disaster is not the direct pro duct of the communist system hut of the bad technical and temperamental conditions of the men who put it into practice And this is not true Wherever commu nism has been imposed whoever may have been the rulers the only thing it has produced is the vanishing of liberties religious persecution and in the social and economic aspect the generalization of misery Of this consists communist equality in imposing misery for all Of course this misery does not reach the upper class the eommunist aristocracy the new class the one which exploits the majorities in the name of the State The red oligarchs live very well they have at their disposal all
